Dollomaja2025: They generated and evaluated an openly available cohort of 30 virtual epilepsy patients from real drug-resistant patients' data, simulating seizures validated against empirical intracranial recordings using ground-truth epileptogenic-zone parameters.
Azilinon2024: This study used quantitative 23Na-MRI features as priors in the Virtual Epileptic Patient pipeline to estimate epileptogenic zone networks, achieving better balanced accuracy than previously used or no priors.
Cai2024: Using a coupled neural mass model fitted to SEEG from twelve refractory focal epilepsy patients, this study simulated virtual radiofrequency thermocoagulation, predicting surgical outcomes with 83.33% AUC and 91.67% accuracy.
Dollomaja2023: Using The Virtual Brain and the Epileptor, the authors built a personalized whole-brain model of status epilepticus from one patient's data, reproducing SEEG patterns and finding propagation depends on connectome and network state.
Wang2023: The Virtual Epileptic Patient workflow used personalized whole-brain models and Bayesian inference to estimate epileptogenic zone networks in 53 drug-resistant focal epilepsy patients, reproducing clinically defined networks with 0.6 precision.
Makhalova2022: Virtual epileptic patient modeling, applied retrospectively to 53 drug-resistant epilepsy patients, detected epileptogenic regions with 64% precision and 44% recall, with higher precision (77%) in seizure-free patients.
Vattikonda2021: Using Bayesian inference, the authors optimized the Epileptor model to build patient-specific virtual models of seizure propagation, validating them on synthetic data and 25 epilepsy patients with varying surgical outcomes.
Courtiol2020: Using The Virtual Brain and an extended Epileptor, this study modeled epileptic and healthy brains, finding interictal resting-state epileptic brains have lower global excitability but locally hyperexcitable epileptogenic regions.
An2019: Using The Virtual Brain, the authors developed a personalized in-silico network approach combining modularity analysis and patient-specific brain network simulation to identify effective, safe surgical interventions outside the epileptogenic zone.
Proix2018: This study introduced a new neural field model of seizure dynamics whose two mechanisms, excitable-media wave propagation and coupled-oscillator dynamics across multiple time scales, explained seizure propagation and termination diversity, confirmed in epilepsy patient data.
Jirsa2017: Using a bitemporal epilepsy patient, this study built a personalized Virtual Epileptic Patient brain model integrating structural connectivity, epileptogenic zone, and MRI lesions, then fit and validated it against empirical SEEG data.
Proix2017: Using diffusion-MRI-derived patient-specific virtual brain models, the authors simulated individual seizure propagation in 15 drug-resistant epilepsy patients, accounting for propagation patterns and postsurgical success variability.
Amato2025: The DADD digital-twin model derived personalized AD biomarkers from non-invasive EEG in 124 participants, identifying CSF-positive patients with 88% accuracy and predicting clinical conversions with 87% accuracy.
Amato2024: They developed a personalized cortical model of Alzheimer's neurodegeneration, fitting parameters from individual resting EEG, which outperformed standard EEG analysis in discriminating MCI from healthy subjects and identifying cerebrospinal-fluid AD hallmarks.
MartinezCanada2023: Using 1/f slopes from resting-state EEG/MEG data and computational modeling, the authors developed and tested a method inferring excitation/inhibition alterations, revealing regional abnormal excitatory and inhibitory parameters in Alzheimer's disease.
Monteverdi2023: Using The Virtual Brain to simulate brain networks in Alzheimer's disease and frontotemporal dementia patients versus controls, the study revealed disease-specific connectivity and synaptic parameter differences correlating with individual clinical and pharmacological profiles.
Patow2023: Using a whole-brain dynamic model to fit amyloid-beta and tau effects on excitation-inhibition balance, they found amyloid-beta dominated early stages (MCI) while tau dominated later stages (AD).
Monteverdi2022: Using The Virtual Brain with a Wong-Wang model, this study simulated brain dynamics in neurodegenerative diseases, finding subject-specific excitation/inhibition parameters that differed between phenotypes and improved discrimination when combined with neuropsychological scores.
Triebkorn2022: Combining empirical PET/MRI features from 33 ADNI3 participants with TVB-simulated local field potentials improved machine-learning classification of Alzheimer's disease by about 10% (F1 64.34% vs. 74.28%).
Arbabyazd2021: Using whole-brain network modeling on ADNI and aging datasets, this study inferred virtual FC from SC (and vice versa), showing classifiers trained on virtual connectomes matched empirical-data performance.
Stefanovski2019: Using The Virtual Brain, the authors built personalized virtual brains where PET-derived amyloid-beta modulated excitation-inhibition balance, reproducing EEG slowing in Alzheimer's patients and its potential reversal by memantine.
Penny2018: Penny et al. fitted dynamic causal models to EEG from presymptomatic PSEN1 carriers, finding a hierarchical inter-hemispheric model optimal and that connectivity differences predicted subsequent MMSE trajectories.
Zimmermann2018a: Using The Virtual Brain to model resting-state activity of 124 individuals spanning healthy aging, MCI, and Alzheimer's, the model's parameters correlated significantly with cognition, exceeding empirical connectomes' predictive capacity.
Mana2023: Analyzing resting-state fMRI from 47 schizophrenia patients and 118 controls with whole-brain model perturbations, they found increased globally coherent states and identified temporo-parietal and medial fronto-temporal areas driving pathological dynamics.
Adams2022: Using dynamic causal modeling of EEG and fMRI paradigms in schizophrenia patients, relatives, and controls, characteristic EEG changes were best explained by greater pyramidal-cell self-inhibition, with disinhibition predicting psychotic symptoms.
Yang2015: Using a large-scale connectivity model and resting-state fMRI from 161 schizophrenia patients, they found E/I elevation predicted hyperconnectivity, most prominent in association cortices and correlating with symptoms.
Angiolelli2025: In 10 Parkinsonian patients before and after L-Dopa, this study analyzed EEG and subthalamic deep electrodes and used a novel dopamine-dependent neural-mass model to correctly infer higher ON-state and lower OFF-state dopaminergic tone.
Jung2022: Using a whole-brain dynamical model to generate simulated BOLD signals, this study showed that combining simulated and empirical connectome features, plus behavioural model fitting, improved machine-learning classification of Parkinson's patients versus healthy controls.
Meier2022: This study implemented a detailed spiking basal ganglia model co-simulated with The Virtual Brain's whole-brain mean-field model, showing virtual deep brain stimulation moved a Parkinson's patient's thalamus-basal ganglia firing rates toward healthy levels while altering distributed cortical activity.
Maith2020: They fit a spiking neuro-computational model to BOLD correlations between basal ganglia nuclei, creating subject-specific Parkinson and control models, finding firing-rate and connectivity differences consistent with prior experiments.
Falcon2016a: Using The Virtual Brain to model fMRI in 20 stroke patients and 11 controls, stroke showed reduced conduction velocity, increased local dynamics, and reduced local inhibitory coupling, relating to motor recovery.
Falcon2015: Using The Virtual Brain to simulate BOLD signals for 20 stroke patients and 10 controls, they found the long-range coupling parameter negatively correlated with global efficiency, but not centrality.
Aerts2020: Using The Virtual Brain, this study found brain network model parameters were stable across tumor resection, and virtual neurosurgery on resected connectomes improved post-surgical functional connectivity similarity in only some patients.
Aerts2018: Using The Virtual Brain, the authors simulated brain dynamics in 25 brain tumor patients and 11 controls, finding individually optimized Reduced Wong-Wang parameters improved functional-connectivity prediction and differentiated tumor-affected regions.
MartiJuan2023: Using The Virtual Brain to model 513 multiple sclerosis patients and 208 healthy controls from 7 centers, higher structural-functional connectivity coupling was associated with cognitive impairment (low SDMT scores).
Good2022: Using The Virtual Brain to fit whole-brain resting-state fMRI in 44 TBI patients, the study found lower regional inhibitory connection strengths predicted more symptoms and worse 6-month cognitive outcomes.
Luppi2022a: Using a perturbed whole-brain haemodynamic model with PET GABA and diffusion MRI data, they showed increased local inhibition and randomised connectivity represent distinct paths to unconsciousness in anaesthesia and disorders of consciousness.
An2022: Using high-resolution personalized Virtual Big Brain models reconstructing over 100,000 individually stimulable fiber tracts, this study simulated DBS in treatment-resistant depression, showing stimulation-location-dependent event-related potentials despite validation limitations.
Hofsaehs2026: Using The Virtual Brain's Jansen-Rit model, the study simulated TEPs and showed that introducing GABAergic deficits raised TEP amplitude toward MDD-like pathological levels, suggesting specific inhibitory therapeutic targets.
Naze2025: Combining neuroimaging, behavioural data, and computational modelling, this study found that decreasing ventromedial frontostriatal coupling while increasing dorsolateral cortico-striatal coupling delivered the highest functional improvements in obsessive-compulsive disorder.
